headline_drivers: Fiscal Q4 2026 earnings beat with upside guidance: adjusted EPS $0.93 vs ~$0.88 consensus and revenue $7.48B vs $6.97B (Q4 FY26 report and commentary, 2026-05-06)

Strong FY26 results with visible AI data-center leverage: FY26 net income up ~5% to $880M, net sales up 8% to $27.9B, and Q4 net profit up 12.6% to $250M on 17% revenue growth to $7.5B (company filings and press coverage, 2026-05-06)

Aggressive FY27 guide well ahead of Street: projected FY27 revenue $32.3B–$33.8B (+~18% midpoint vs FY26) and adjusted EPS $4.21–$4.51, tying multi‑year growth to cloud & AI infrastructure, power, and autos (earnings call / guidance, 2026-05-06)

Announced spin-off of high-growth Cloud & Power Infrastructure business ("SpinCo") into a separate public company by Q1 2027, aimed at unlocking AI data-center valuation similar to Vertiv/nVent peers (spinoff announcement with earnings, 2026-05-06)

Street re‑rating and momentum: multiple analysts raising price targets (e.g., Baird from $70 to $88 with Outperform) and commentary positioning FLEX as an AI data center and power infrastructure beneficiary rather than a low-multiple EMS (analyst notes following Q4 print, 2026-05-06 to 2026-05-07)

Technical and momentum follow‑through: stock gapped from mid‑$90s to >$120 intraday and closed at $134.73 on 2026-05-06 (+~39%), with continued high-volume trading and valuation re‑rating to >40x P/E as traders price in AI infrastructure growth and spin-related sum‑of‑parts upside (price/volume action and media recaps, 2026-05-06 to 2026-05-07)
summary: The move in FLEX is being driven by a classic re‑rating around a clean earnings beat plus a structural story change. On May 6, Flex reported a Q4 FY26 beat and strong FY26 numbers, then issued FY27 revenue and EPS guidance well above consensus, explicitly tying growth to AI data‑center, cloud, power, and auto demand. At the same time, management announced a planned spin‑off of its high‑growth Cloud & Power Infrastructure unit into a standalone public company by Q1 2027, which investors see as unlocking a higher AI‑infrastructure multiple than Flex’s legacy EMS business would command. Analysts quickly raised targets and reframed FLEX as an AI data‑center infrastructure and power play, and the stock has since traded like a momentum leader, with the recent pullback likely just digestion after a sharp, volume‑backed breakout rather than a change in the thesis.
